title: store: COMMIT issued even when inner async block returns Ok but result may be a soft-success lie
description: The pattern used throughout store.rs (try_set_nickname, save_key_package_capped, enqueue, drain, add_group_member_capped, remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group, etc.) commits the transaction when result.is_ok() and rolls back on Err. However some paths return Ok(false) or Ok(AlreadyMember) as soft-no-op outcomes that should still commit (they are correct). The real risk is the inverse: if any of those inner blocks return Ok but have silently skipped a write (due to INSERT OR IGNORE), the COMMIT is issued and no rollback occurs. This is by design for idempotency in most places, but is load-bearing for correctness proofs — no single issue here beyond the specific rows_affected checks above. This issue tracks the pattern for future audit.
close_reason: informational pattern audit; specific instances fixed in nie-qmwv.1 and nie-qmwv.2
